advisory.title: Gateway device.pair.approve Lets operator.pairing Escalate a New Device into operator.admin, reaching Node RCE.
advisory.description:
## Summary

device.pair.approve allowed an operator.pairing approver to approve a pending device request for broader operator scopes than the approver actually held.

## Affected Packages / Versions

- Package: `openclaw` (npm)
- Affected: `< 2026.3.22`
- Fixed: `>= 2026.3.22`
- Latest released tag checked: `v2026.3.23-2` (`630f1479c44f78484dfa21bb407cbe6f171dac87`)

## Fix

Fixed by commit `fc2d29ea926f47c428c556e92ec981441228d2a4`.

## Code-Level Confirmation

- `src/gateway/server-methods/devices.ts` now threads caller scopes into `device.pair.approve`.
- `src/infra/device-pairing.ts` now rejects requested operator scopes that exceed the approver-held operator scope set.

timeline.notes: The gateway RPC approval path and scope-bearing device-pairing flow land in feat: unify device auth + pairing. vulnerableHead stays on the last pre-fix snapshot immediately before fc2d29ea926f47c428c556e92ec981441228d2a4.

expectedOutcome.description: Scanner should detect brokenauthz when scanning vulnerableHead.

checks.name: vulnerable_head_is_fix_parent
checks.details: Using parent 132e45900904fc981e6ef04259eb37c72f6c165d of fix commit fc2d29ea926f47c428c556e92ec981441228d2a4 as the last known vulnerable commit.
